title: Datalog Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Datalog syntax and commands for engineers

commands.description: Define a simple fact in Datalog

commands.description: Define a rule with a single condition
commands.scenario: Define a rule with a single condition

commands.command: ancestor(X, Y) :- parent(X, Z), ancestor(Z, Y).
commands.description: Define a rule with multiple conditions
commands.scenario: Define a rule with multiple conditions

commands.description: Query for all parents of 'doe'
Query for all ancestors of 'doe'
Query with a variable condition

commands.command: reachable(X, Y) :- edge(X, Y). reachable(X, Y) :- edge(X, Z), reachable(Z, Y).
commands.description: Define a recursive rule
Use negation in a rule

commands.command: sibling(X, Y) :- parent(Z, X), parent(Z, Y), X \= Y.
commands.description: Define a rule with disjunction
Use the 'not' operator in a query

commands.description: Check for equality in a query
commands.scenario: Check for equality in a query

commands.command: age_difference(X, Y, D) :- age(X, A1), age(Y, A2), D is A1 - A2.
commands.description: Use arithmetic in a rule
Use indexing to speed up queries
commands.scenario: Use indexing to speed up queries

commands.command: % Use positive logic where possible to optimize performance
commands.description: Avoid using negation in large datasets
commands.scenario: Avoid using negation in large datasets

commands.command: % Break complex rules into simpler sub-rules for clarity and performance
commands.description: Simplify rules by breaking them into smaller parts
commands.scenario: Simplify rules by breaking them into smaller parts

commands.description: Explicitly declare relations with specific types to enable static type checking and optimize memory layout.
Direct the engine to stream the results of a specific relation directly to a file or stdout for large dataset processing.
Generate and inspect the query execution plan to identify join order inefficiencies and unexpected nested loops.
Use constant binding in the first subgoal of a rule to prune the search space early (Magic Sets optimization pattern).
Restrict the engine to only find the first N satisfying tuples, useful for quick exploratory queries on massive datasets.
